_doc: Asset-tagging configuration for SecurityInsight. Copy this file to 'asset-tagging.config.json' in the same directory (gitignored) and customize. The tagging engine reads the JSON at startup. Two kinds of customisation live here: (1) per-rule on/off gates via 'AutoExcludeRules.*'; (2) device-name filter patterns consumed ONLY by the 'Auto-NamePattern--Excluded--SI' rule (not ANDed with the 3 OnboardingStatus rules).

AutoExcludeRules._doc: Four independent rules. Each flag, when true, makes the tagging engine run that rule and emit an MDE machine tag of '<bucket>--Excluded--SI' on every device matching the rule's criteria. Rules are NOT ANDed -- a device that matches multiple rules gets multiple tags (which is fine; downstream RiskAnalysisGlobalExclusions.custom.json's ExcludedAssetTags: ['--Excluded--SI'] keys off the SUFFIX, not the prefix). Engine runs each rule as its own Defender Graph query + its own bulk MDE tag pass.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeCanBeOnboardedDoc: When true, tag every ExposureGraphNodes device whose rawData.onboardingStatus is 'canBeOnboarded' (Defender sees the device but Sense agent isn't installed yet). No name scoping -- the WHOLE bucket gets the Auto-CanBeOnboarded--Excluded--SI tag.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeInsufficientInfoDoc: When true, tag every ExposureGraphNodes device whose rawData.onboardingStatus is 'insufficientInfo'. No name scoping.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeUnsupportedDoc: When true, tag every ExposureGraphNodes device whose rawData.onboardingStatus is 'unsupported' (legacy / appliance OS Defender can't onboard at all). No name scoping.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeByDeviceNamePatternDoc: When true, run the Auto-NamePattern--Excluded--SI rule. The rule pulls every ExposureGraphNodes device and the engine post-filters rows against DeviceNamePatterns.{StartsWith, Contains, MatchesRegex} below. INDEPENDENT of OnboardingStatus -- e.g. 'desktop-*' boxes get tagged regardless of whether they're already onboarded. Leave false (or remove the entry) to disable name-pattern-based exclusion entirely.

DeviceNamePatterns._doc: Patterns consumed exclusively by the Auto-NamePattern--Excluded--SI rule (gated by AutoExcludeRules.ExcludeByDeviceNamePattern). When ALL three arrays are empty, the rule effectively matches every device (intended -- lets you ship the flag with patterns added later). When any one is populated, a device matches if its DeviceName satisfies ANY entry across ANY array (OR semantics). The 3 OnboardingStatus rules above DO NOT read these patterns.
